def: s.m. The letter or sound au.

ety: of وبش, per met. for بوش, not in use
def: s.m. (orig.), A mixed company or crowd of people (especially of the meanest sort), mob; canaille; — a bad character, dissolute fellow, profligate, debauchee, rake, libertine; — adj. Dissolute, profligate, rakish, lecherous; — aubāsh honā, To become depraved or dissolute by low company.
adj. Like a rake; rakish, lissolute.
s.f. Depravity, profligacy, dissipation, dissoluteness, debauchery, rakishness, lewdness; fondness for attending games and spectacles.
adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)
adj. = aubaṭ, q.v. adj. Rough, rugged, uneven, impassable, impracticable (a road); steep, inaccessible, &c. ( = aughaṭ, q.v.)
adv. Unexpectedly, suddenly, unawares ( = aćānćak and aćānak, q.q.v.).
adv. Unexpectedly, suddenly, unawares ( = aćānćak and aćānak, q.q.v.).
s.m. The plant Morinda tinctoria (or M. citrifolia), from the root of which an orange colour is extracted ( = āć).
s.m. Fitness, suitableness, aptness, propriety, rightness.
s.m. Returning; a return; a relapse; — a man advanced in years, a man of experience and wisdom, or of ability and prudence
subs: v.n. To return or revert (to), to fall back (upon); to have a relapse (of).

def: s.m. That which is thrown in or given over and above what is purchased, to boot, gratis.
s.m. Generosity, liberality, munificence.
s.f. Ill, evil, misfortune, calamity.
s.m. Loneliness, solitude.
adj. & s.m. White; grey; — white or grey colour (cf. ūdā).
s.m. Foreign country, strange land = pardes and udes, q.q.v. s.m. Quicksilver, mercury; — adj. Heavy, weighty
subs: pārā pilānā (meṅ, -ko), To fill or load with quicksilver; to make heavy

def: s.m. Foreign country, strange land = pardes and udes, q.q.v. s.m. Quicksilver, mercury; — adj. Heavy, weighty
subs: pārā pilānā (meṅ, -ko), To fill or load with quicksilver; to make heavy

def: s.m. Foreigner, stranger = pardesī (the more com. term).

adj. = auṅdhā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), With the face down; upset, upside down, overturned, inverted, topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, ruined; adverse (as fortune); crooked, distorted; — s.m. Blockhead, fool
subs: v.n. To lie on the face
adj. Crooked-headed; ill-fated, unlucky, unfortunate; shortsighted
adv. On the face, with the face or head down; head-foremost, headlong
lit. 'To suck with the face down'; to act like a child, to be silly, ignorant, &c.

def: adj. Deep, unfathomable.
adj. Ill-formed, awkward, ungainly, uncouth, &c. ( = angaṛh, q.v.); — adv. Awkwardly, &c.
adj. Rough, uneven, impassable, impracticable (as a road), difficult of access, steep, inaccessible; unfrequented (cf. aubaṭ and ūbaṛ).
s.f. A driving whip, a whip like a waggoner's (about seven cubits long, used in training horses); — an ornamental edging of superior country shoes (peculiar to Dehli); — a reel or skein.
s.f. A pit or hole for catching elephants, tigers, &c. ( = obhī, q.v.).
adj. Without virtue or science, ignorant, worthless, vicious, depraved, corrupt; — s.m. A vicious or corrupt person.
s.m. Highest point, top, summit, vertex; zenith; height, altitude, ascendancy; highest apsis of a planet; highest position, rank, or dignity; preferment, promotion; prosperity
subs: To attain to the summit of good fortune, &c.; to be very prosperous; to obtain the highest position, or dignity.

def: adj. Restless, uneasy ( = be-kal)
adj. Untimely, unseasonable ( = a-samay); born before its time (a child), base-born (child).
s.f. Restlessness, uneasiness ( = be-kalī).
adj. Untimely, unseasonable ( = a-samay); born before its time (a child), base-born (child).
s.f. (pl. of waqt), Times, hours; circumstances, state, condition; means, resources, power, ability
subs: (-se), To pass (one's) days (in), to subsist (upon, or by means of), to earn a livelihood, to live
s.f. Passing (one's) time, occupation, employment, livelihood, means of living
auqāt-basarī kāṭnā = auqāt basar karnā, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَوْقَات (ʔawqāt), the plural of وَقْت (waqt), whence Urdu وَقْت (vaqt, “time”). Although this is the formal plural of vaqt, it is generally used as a singular feminine noun with a different sense from "time".

def: s.f. = aushadhi, q.v.
s.m. Any great calamity, (as a) plague, cholera, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: adj. = اولٰي aulā, q.v.
adj. (compar. & superl. of walī), Better; best; superior, excellent
adj. & s.m. = ava-lakshaṇ, q.v. adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
s.f. Legitimate children
subs: s.f. Children in a directline, lineal descendants
s.f. Legitimate children or issue ( = aulāduʼl-ḥalāl)
s.f. Legitimate issue from both sides (paternal and maternal)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوْلَاد (awlād, “offspring, child(ren), descendant”), from Arabic أَوْلَاد (ʔawlād, “children”), plural of وَلَد (walad, “child; son”).

def: adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
s.f. Superiority, preëminence, excellence (see aulā).
s.m. = ail-fail, q.v. s.m. Abusive language, obscenity; prating, chatter, babble (also aul-faul: used by Mohammadan women).
s.m. Friends (of God), saints, holy men, prophets, apostles; a saint ( = walī); the holy; — (iron.) a godless person.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوْلِیَاء (awliyā'), from Arabic أَوْلِيَاء (ʔawliyāʔ).

def: adj. Strange, odd, singular, unusual; unpleasant, wearisome, dull; uneasy, restless, perturbed; — s.m. Strangeness, oddness, newness; unpleasantness, dullness, irksomeness; uneasiness, restlessness.
s.m. Aid, help, assistance; — aider, helper; defender, protector, guard.
adj. (f. -ī), Deep ( = gahrā); sunk.
s.f. Depth.
adj. (f. -ī), With the face down; upset, upside down, overturned, inverted, topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, ruined; adverse (as fortune); crooked, distorted; — s.m. Blockhead, fool
subs: v.n. To lie on the face
adj. Crooked-headed; ill-fated, unlucky, unfortunate; shortsighted
adv. On the face, with the face or head down; head-foremost, headlong
lit. 'To suck with the face down'; to act like a child, to be silly, ignorant, &c.

def: v.t. To turn upside down, turn over, upset, invert, overturn, reverse; to pour out (water), empty (a vessel); to hang down (the head)
v.n. To be overturned, to turn upside down, upset; to be poured out; to lower (as the sky).
adv. At whatever can be got, at a loss.
s.f. Silence, dumbness.
s.f. & adv. Roving about, rambling, sauntering.
s.m. = آنولا āṅʼolā, q.v.
v.t. To boil, &c. = auṭānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
s.f. = auṭāʼonī, q.v. s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk is boiled.
v.n. To be boiled, &c.
adj. And, also, for the rest, besides, again, moreover; but, yet, still; over, else; and lo!; — another, other, different; more, additional
subs: Others, others besides; other kinds of
And again, and more, still more, also, moreover
Others apart, others being put aside
All other, all the others, the rest
One word more; — only hear! just listen!
Anyone else, any other, another, different ( = koʼī aur)
Anything else, some other, some or something more; something different ( = kućh aur)
What else? what more? what next? — certainly, assuredly, of course
And if not then; and otherwise
And this that; moreover, besides that.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: s.m. pl. (of wird), Daily rehearsals (of the Qǒrʼān), commemorations, devotional exercises.
s.m. pl. (of waraq), Leaves (of a tree, &c.); pages.
s.m. A throne; — places where goods are manufactured for sale ( = S. avaḍranga)
subs: lit. 'Ornament of the throne'; name of an emperor of Hindūstūn (who died in 1707)
s.f. A kind of cloth; — a kind of eruption (said to have broken out in the reign of Aurangzeb)

def: s.m.f. Legitimate, legitimately born; — a legitimate child (i.e. by a wife of the same tribe).
adv. All round, about, around; lengthwise and breadthwise; — adj. Level, even ( = ćauras, q.v.).

ety: for A. عورة, v.n. fr. عور 'to be blind,' &c.
def: s.f. (orig.) The private part or parts (so called because it is abominable to uncover or expose them); — (in Urdū) a woman; a wife
ex: یہ ہم گنہ گار عورتیں ہیں جو اہل جبہ کی تمکنت سے نہ رعب کھائیں نہ جان بیچیں نہ سر جھکائیں نہ ہاتھ جوڑیں
ye ham gunahgār aurtẽ ha͠i jo ahl-e-jubbā kī tamaknat se na rob khāẽ na jān becẽ na sar jhukāẽ na hāth joṛẽ
It is we sinful women who are not awed by the grandeur of those who wear gowns, who don't sell our lives, who don't bow our heads, who don't fold our hands together
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عَوْرَت (awrat), from Arabic عَوْرَة (ʕawra, “genitals; nudity”), where the shift to "woman" in early New Indo-Aryan happened because it was considered immodest to expose the female body in public. First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi عَورَت ('vrt)/औरत (aurata).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻaurat), Private parts, pudenda; women (the com. signification in Urdū).

ety: ʻaurat, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Of or relating to woman; like a woman.
s.m. Any rite performed in honour of the dead; obsequies; funeral solemnities.
adj. Crooked, deformed, badly-formed, ugly; inverted, transposed; — s.m. Crookedness, deceit ( = fareb)
adj. Mad, crazy, &c. = bāʼolā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Mad, crazy, deranged, demented, insane.
s.m. Submarine fire.
s.m. Rice sown in April-May and growing luxuriantly during the rainy season.
s.m. Noble properties or attributes, praiseworthy qualifications, &c.
s.m. Calmness, coolness, self-possession, presence of mind, courage; sense, senses; sensation
subs: ausān jāte-rahnā, To lose (one's) presence of mind, to lose (one's) senses, to be stupefied, to stand aghast

def: adj. Middle, middling, intervening, intermediate; average; — s.m. (f.?), Middle, medium, mean, average interval; mediocrity
adj. Midmost, middling, moderate; pertaining to the average.
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Care, solicitude, anxiety. (Also written अवसेर.)
s.f. Medicine, medicament, drug, any herb, mineral, &c. used in medicine
adj. Medicinal; — s.m. Ingredient of a medicine; materia medica.
s.f. Secretion or rheum of the eyes.
s.m. pl. (of waṣī), Executors, administrators of the divine will, or of the will of mortals, legatees; preceptors, guardians.

ety: aus˚ = Prk. ओवस(इ) or अववस(इ) = S. अपवस(ति), rt. अप+वस्
def: v.n. To become musty; sticky, &c. = ubasnā, q.v. v.n. To give forth a smell, become fetid; to turn; to smell offensive (meat or food); to rot, putrefy, become rancid or rank; to become mouldy, musty, or mildewed; to ferment; to become sticky or viscous; to become stale, or useless; to be weary, to be out of patience.
adj. Without consideration, thoughtless, heedless, unconcerned.
s.m. Defunct person ( = pret); spirit of a deceased person; one who dies without leaving issue; an unmarried man; stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead, fool ( = mūrkh).
s.m. pl. (of watad), Pegs, pins; props; chiefs, nobles; certain four saints (regarded as the props of the faith).
s.m. Boiling; evaporation by boiling; — a knife used for cutting tobacco.

ety: caus. of auṭnā or auṅṭnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To cause to boil; to boil, boil down; to heat.
s.f. An earthen vessel in which milk is boiled.
s.m. Excellence, superiority.
s.m. Excellence, superiority.

ety: auṭ˚ = Prk. ओउट्ट(इ) = S. अवकुट्य(ति), rt. अव+कुट्
def: v.t. To boil; to evaporate over a fire; — v.n. To boil; to be hot; to be consumed with rage or vexation. (See auṅṭnā.)
s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.
s.m. Anxiety, disquietude, perturbation, uneasiness, regret; ardent desire, longing, eagerness, impatience.

ety: inf. n. fr. عوذ 'to seek protection,' &c.
def: s.m. Seeking (God's) protection; fleeing to for refuge; refuge, asylum.
s.f. pl. (of وضع q.v.), Behaviour, manners, deportment, address, breeding, politeness; gestures, postures: — auẓāʻ-at̤wār = auẓāʻ.
s.m. pl. (of wazn), Weights, measures, metres.
(pl. of وزر), s.m. sing. & plur. Tool, instrument; arms, tools, instruments, apparatus, &c. = ālāt, q.v.
s.m. Potter's kiln; kiln; furnace.

s.m. A kiln, &c. = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)

ety: perf. part. of āʼonā = ānā, 'to come'
def: s.m. Coming, approach
subs: s.m. = āwā-jāwī, s.f. = āwā-jāʼī, s.f. Coming and going; intercourse, communication; visiting, interchange of visits; access; dancing attendance (upon).

def: s.m. Cover, covering.
s.m. Boundary, limit; distinction.
adj. Not to be spoken of; vile, bad; — silent, dumb.
corr. from ābādān, q.v.
s.f. = ābādānī, q.v. s.f. A habitation; a cultivated, populous, or pleasant place; population; cultivation; abundance; prosperity; advancement; civilization; enjoyment, pleasure.
v.t. To forsake ( = tyāg karnā).
prop. n. The province of Oude.
adj. Attentive, careful, cautious; — s.m. Attention, care, caution, vigilance; staring at.
s.m. Compassion towards the lowly.
s.m. Ascertainment; affirmation, emphasis; stating or holding positively; accurate determination; limitation, restriction (as to sense, instances, &c.).
s.f. Unobstructedness.
adv. As far as; as long as.
s.m. A kind of religious mendicant (see abdhūt).
adj. Not to be killed, inviolable; not fit to be killed.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Gūjarātī Brāhmans.
s.m. Bathing, ablution, dip, dive, plunge.
s.m. Bathing, ablution, dip, dive, plunge.
s.m. Coming and going; intercourse, &c. ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāʼā, &c.); ingress and egress; being born and dying; transmigration.
part. Known, understood, comprehended.
s.f. Perception, comprehension, knowledge.
s.m. Coming and going; intercourse, &c. ( = ānā-jānā, āwā-jāʼā, &c.); ingress and egress; being born and dying; transmigration.
part. adj. Detested; blamed, reproached; wicked, vile; — s.m. Blame, reproach, obloquy; satire.
s.m. Defect, blemish, fault, demerit, worthlessness, evil, vice; harm, mischief; contempt, disregard ( = S. avagan); a corrupt or vicious person ( = augunī)
subs: (-ko), To impute a fault, vice, &c. (to), to slander, defame.

def: s.f. Disregard, disrespect, contempt, despite
subs: adj. Contemptuous, degrading, derogatory; — s.m. A contemptuous person, &c.

def: s.m. Sending for, calling, summoning, inviting; call, summons; invitation.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.f. Approach, advance; proximity; arrival; report or news of (one's) approach or arrival ( = āvātī); report, rumour, hearsay ( = afwāh); — a saddle-cloth adorned with fringes; — a kind of pickaxe.
s.m. pl. (of ʻaib, q.v.), Blemishes, defects; faults, vices.
s.m. (f. ?), Beginnings; beginning, first part, early portion or period; the first ten days of a lunar month (the remaining decades are termed awāsit and awāḵẖir, q.q.v.)
subs: awāʼil-ʻumr, s.f. Early youth, tender age, child hood; — s.m. A youth
adv. In the beginning at first.

def: adj. (f. awaiʼī), Future, yet to come
s.m. Insurance (syn. bīmā).
part. Ascertained, concluded, certain.
s.m. Leisure, opportunity ( = ausar); interval, space, room, way, scope.
adj. (f. inī), Unfruitful, barren.
s.m. plur. (of āḵẖir), Ends, latter parts, extremes; the last ten days of a lunar month.
s.f. pl. (of ʻāqibat, q.v.), Ends, &c.; consequences; — posterity.
s.m. Enclosed space formed by a cluster of peasant's houses; a string fastened round the wheel of a distaff.
adj. Of inauspicious or evil omen; — unfortunate; — s.m. Bad omen.
adj. Hanging down, depending, hanging on or upon; — s.m. Asylum, protection, dependence, support.
s.m. Depending (upon), supporting oneself (upon); dependence, reliance; support, prop.
— see اولمب avalamb.
adj. Depending or hanging (on), dependent (on); delaying, dilatory; — s.m. Dependent; refugee; procrastinator.
part. adj. Suspended (from), depending (on), trusting (to), supported (by); protected, cherished; alighting, descending.
s.f. Row, range, continuous line, series; lineage, dynasty
s.m. Looking at, beholding; sight, view, inspection.
part. Seen, looked at, inspected; — s.m. A saint of the Jain sect.
v.t. To look at, see, inspect, view.
s.m. Leaping suddenly (upon), breaking forth, rushing on or away
s.m. Debt, loan; — colour (see wām).
adj. Undermost, inferior, lowest, base, vile, low; growing less, decreasing; — s.m. A lunar day exactly coinciding with a solar one.
s.m. pl. (of A. عامة ʻāmmat, fr. ʻām, q.v.), The commonalty, the common people, the common sort, the vulgar, the populace
subs: The common people, the populace at large, the public.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عوام ('awām), from Arabic عَوَامّ (ʕawāmm), the plural of عَامَّة (ʕāmma). Compare Punjabi ਅਵਾਮ (avām).

def: s.m. Kiln = āwā, q.v. A Sanskrit prefix denoting 'from,' 'down from,' 'off,' 'away.' (It is the reverse of आ ā, and is sometimes used to denote negation. deprivation, disgrace, disjunction, &c.)
s.m. Coming, approach, &c. ( = awāʼī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Tidings or news of arrival.

def: s.m. The field into which rice has been transplanted (cf. āman).
s.m. Iron band round the navs of a wheel.
s.f. plur. (of ān, 'time'), Times, seasons.
s.m. pl. (of عون ʻaun), Aids, helps; assistants, aiders.
s.m. Vessel, pan.
see s.v. āwanā or āʼonā.
s.f. The earth, world; — avanī-pa, s.m. Lord of the earth (an epithet of a king or ruler).
s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
s.f. Name of a city, the modern Oujain.
s.f. Delay; — adv. Late, &c. = aver, q.v. s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
subs: awerī-sawerī, adv. Late and early, at all hours, unseasonably; late or early, late in the evening or early in the morning
v.n. To delay, loiter, dally, to procrastinate.

def: s.m. Account-book, &c. = āmār and avārija, q.q.v.; also = āwāra, q.v. s.m. Heap, pile. (The word would appear to be peculiar to the Panjāb.)
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).
s.m. Injustice, tyranny ( = S. अपकार).

r: āwar, āwardan, ā, war, bar, zor-āwar
ety: rt. of āwardan; ā+war = Zend bar = S. भर्], Bringing, bearing; possessing, endowed with (used as last member of compounds, e.g. zor-āwar
def: adj. Having strength, strong, powerful).
adj. Low, inferior, hinder; — s.m. Low person; a śūdra.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: adj. Separated from one's family ( = judā); without house and home; wandering, roving; astray; abandoned, lost; dissolute; — s.m. Wanderer; vagabond; profligate
subs: To wander or rove about; to wander astray; to go loafing about
(-ko), To drive out of house and home; to make a vagabond of; to ruin
To be separated from one's family, &c. (see āwāra).

def: s.f. Wandering; vagrancy; profligacy.
s.m. Covering; a covering, shield, protection; obstruction, lock.
adj. Not natural, artificial, false; affected; assumed (opp. to āmad, 'that which comes natural').
s.f. Period of life, &c. = āyurdī, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of āwardan; see āwar
def: s.m. That which is brought or carried over
subs: one who is taken into favour
s.m. Registrar or writer of accounts as delivered.

def: adj. (in Gram.), Not classified.
s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
s.m. pl. of ʻāriẓ, or ʻāriẓa, qq.v.
s.m. = āwārija, q.v. s.m. Account-book; day-book; note-book, diary, journal; book of receipts and disbursements.
s.m. Stoppage, obstruction, hindrance, resistance, restraint, confinement; — the seraglio of a palace, the queen's apartment
s.m. Absence of impediment, non-opposition, assent, concurrence; absence of contention, tranquillity, quiet.
s.m. Turning; turn; curl; whirlpool, eddy.
part. Hindered, restrained, stopped, obstructed, kept in order, controlled; besieged, imprisoned, shut up, confined, secluded.
s.m. House, dwelling, habitation.
adv. Certainly, &c. = avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
adj. Unsubmissive to another's will, not under control, unsubdued, unrestrained, disobedient; — not having one's own free will, subject to another, without choice, helpless, powerless.
s.m. End, conclusion, completion; boundary, limit; death, destruction.
s.m. Occasion, fit moment, time or season (of), favourable opportunity; vacation, leisure
subs: To find occasion, get an opportunity; to have or find leisure
To watch for an opportunity, to observe the time or season (of); to bide (one's) time.

def: s.m. Residue, &c. = avaśesh, q.v.
s.m. Residue, remainder, surplus; end, close, termination, appendix, supplement (syn. tatimma).
s.f. Unripe corn (chiefly barley) cut from time to time and brought home to be eaten.
= avaśya, q.v. adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀑𑀲𑀸 (osā), from Sanskrit अवश्या (avaśyā). Cognate with Bengali ওস (ōs).

def: part. = ava-śishṭ.
part. Left, remaining.
s.m. pl. (of ausat̤), Middles, means, averages; the period of a lunar month between the tenth and twentieth days.
s.f. State, condition, circumstances ( = ḥāl); age; life; story, narrative (of a life).
adj. Unreal, unsubstantial, vain, trifling; unessential, not belonging to the true nature of a thing; — s.m. A worthless thing, nothing, &c.
s.f. Unreality, insubstantiality; non-essentialness.
adj. Necessary, &c. = avaśyak; — adv. Certainly, assuredly, of course, to be sure, it is true, inevitably, indispensably
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
s.m. Anything necessary, necessity, expediency; — adj. Necessary, indispensable, inevitable, actual.
s.f. Necessity, need, indispensableness, inevitability.
s.m. Crest; ear-ring; ornament, gem; wealth; halter, rein.

s.m. Descent (especially of a deity from heaven), appearance of any deity upon earth; incarnation (more particularly of Vishṇu in ten principal forms, viz. 1˚ Matsya, 'the fish'; 2˚ Kaććhapa, 'the tortoise'; 3˚ Varāha, 'the boar'; 4˚ Nr̤i-singha, 'the man-lion'; 5˚ Vāmana, 'the dwarf'; 6˚ Paraśu-rāma; 7˚ Rāma; 8˚ Balarāma, or Kr̤ishna; 9˚ Buddhā; 10˚ Kalkī); — any new and unexpected appearance or phenomenon; any pious or distinguished person (in the language of respect or flattery); a wicked, depraved or quarrelsome person (ironically); — crossing ( = utār); translation; a tīrth or sacred place of Hindū pilgrimage
subs: autār lenā, v.n. To become incarnate, to appear in the world (a deity).

def: v.n. To descend (especially as an incarnation of the deity = autār lenā); to cross, &c. = utarnā, q.v.
= avatarnā, q.v.
s.f. = awātī, q.v. s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).
s.f. Approach, coming; advance; coming of an army; approach of a friend; season in which merchandise is expected (cf. awāʼī).
part. Descended; crossed, passed over; translated.
s.f. = ʻaf-ʻaf, q.v. s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
s.m. A limb, member; part, portion; member or component part of a logical argument or syllogism.

ety: Pehl. āfāj, Zend ā+vać, S. वाच्
def: s.f. Sound, noise; voice, tone; whisper; echo; shout, call, cry; report, fame; sentence
subs: To raise the voice, to speak out; to strain the voice; to spread a report
āwāz ūṅćī baṛhānā, To raise the voice ( = āwāz uṭhānā)
āwāz paṛnā, or paṛ-jānā, To become hoarse from cold, singing, &c.; to lose the voice; — āwāz-baiṭhā, adj. Hoarse
or rakhnā, or lagānā, To lend an attentive ear, to hear, listen
(kān-meṅ), To happen to hear, to catch a sound; to overhear; to be heard, to be reported
(-kī), To answer to, or to obey, a call
(-ko), To call out (to), shout (to), to hail; — v.n. To give forth a sound; to answer a call
āwāz-kā ṭappā, s.m. Range or reach of voice; pitch of the voice; hearing-distance, ear-shot
(-kī), To bring forth a sound or report (from), cause to sound; to fire off (a gun, &c.)
To call or cry out (one's wares); to beg; to raise the voice (in singing)
To sing in harmony or concert (with)
To give out a sound; to bring forth or produce a sound; to speak.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آواز (āvāz), from Middle Persian ʾwʾz (āwāz). Cognate with Sanskrit वाच् (vā́c) and Latin vōx. First attested in c. 1665 as Middle Hindi آواز (āvaz).

def: s.m. Noise; loud talk = āwāz; report; rumour, fame
subs: āwāza kasnā, āwāza mārnā (-par), To talk (of), make insinuations (about); to speak in innuendoes; to jeer (at)

def: s.m. The ocean = abdhi, q.v. s.m. The ocean. — abdhi-kaph, s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.

ety: perf. part. of āweḵẖtan, Zend ā+rt. vij; S. ā+vij or vić
def: part. adj. Suspended, hanging, pendulous, pendent; attached, connected.
s.f. Delay, &c.; — adv. Late, &c. (see aber)
subs: awerī-sawerī, adv. Late and early, at all hours, unseasonably; late or early, late in the evening or early in the morning
v.n. To delay, loiter, dally, to procrastinate.

def: adj. Hanging, &c. ( = āweḵẖta, q.v.)
subs: (-par), To hang (upon), suspend (a thing, from), attach (to), fix (upon), to post (a notice).

def: s.m. Pendant, ornament for the ear, ear-ring
adj. Hanging, &c. ( = āweḵẖta, q.v.)
subs: (-par), To hang (upon), suspend (a thing, from), attach (to), fix (upon), to post (a notice).

def: s.f. Hanging, suspension (used in comp.).
s.m. Sun; mountain; preservation; sheep; shawl-goat.
adj. (in law), Indivisible (as property, &c.).
part. adj. Undivided, unseparated; joint; not sharing.
adj. Motionless; unmoved, unshaken, firm, resolute.
s.m. Want of consideration or judgment; injustice. (See a-bićār.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Destitute of consideration, wanting in judgment, irreflective; unjust; — an irreflective person; an ill-advised person.
part. Unconsidered, ill-judged, ill-advised, not investigated, undiscussed.
adj. Without interval, close, dense, coarse, uninterrupted.
adj. Not known, not understood.
s.f. Ignorance, stupidity
subs: adj. Unlearned, illiterate, ignorant; — s.m. An illiterate person.

def: adj. Diffusive, pervading, spreading or extending widely.
adj. Not understood, not known, not famous or celebrated.
adj. Unimpaired, perfect, entire; regular, orderly, consistent.
adj. Without change, not liable to change.
adj. Unchangeable, constant, faithful, true; — s.m. One who does not change, a constant person.
adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
part. adj. Not delaying, &c. = avilamb, q.v. adj. Not delaying, prompt, quick, ready, expeditious; — s.m. Haste, despatch, quickness, diligence.
s.m. Exemption from destruction, exception from loss, safety, perpetuity.
adj. Exempt from liability to destruction or loss, indestructible, imperishable, everlasting, perpetual; safe, entire.
adj. Without proper training, ill-behaved, indecorous; — s.m. Want of good manners or modesty; bad training; rude behaviour, indecorum, impropriety, pertness, wantonness.
adj. Without point or dot.
part. adj. Badly trained, ill-mannered, misbehaving, acting improperly, wicked, vile.
s.f. An immodest or unchaste woman.
adj. Helpless, weak, impotent.
s.f. (of avīr), A childless widow.
adj. Uninterrupted; close, dense; coarse.
Unfolding, developing; development, manifestation; presence, a becoming visible.
part. Unfolded, developed, manifested; apparent.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Not contentious or combative, peaceable, tranquil, quiet: — a peaceable person.
part. adj. Unobstructed, unimpeded; allowed, permitted; compatible; not discordant, harmonious.
adj. Without application; without desire, indifferent; — s.m. An indifferent person, &c.
adv. Without any difference, uniform, alike, like, resembling, undistinguishable, indiscriminate; — s.m. Want of distinction, &c.
part. adj. Not distinguished, not discerned.
adj. Not poisonous; anti-venomous.
s.m. The not being an object of sense; invisibility; — adj. Unacquainted with objects of sense.
adj. Possessed (as by an evil spirit); engrossed (as by a sentiment or feeling).
adj. Mistrusted, distrusted; — s.m. Mistrust, distrust, suspicion, unbelief.
s.m. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness, distrust, &c. = a-viśwās.
adj. & s.m. (f. inī), Wanting in trust, confidence, belief, &c., mistrustful, unfaithful, unbelieving; — a distrustful person; unbeliever, infidel.
s.m. = S اوشوامتا अविश्वासता a-viśwāsatā, s.f. Distrustfulness, suspiciousness, distrust, &c. = a-viśwās.
adj. Not contentious or disputatious, peaceable, conciliatory, gentle; — s.m. A peaceable person, &c.
part. Unmarried, &c. = abi-wāhit, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unmarried, single
subs: a bachelor. — abiwāhitā, s.f. An unmarried woman; a mistress.

def: adj. Without judgment or discrimination ( = avivekī, q.v.); — s.m. Absence of discrimination or judgment, indiscretion, ignorance, stupidity.
s.f. Want of judgment, inconsiderateness, imprudence, &c. ( = avivek).
adj. Undiscriminating, having no judgment, indiscreet, ignorant, superficial, short-sighted; — s.m. An undiscriminating person, &c.
v.n. To come = ānā, q.v.
subs: āwanī-hār, āwane-hār, adj. Coming, having yet to arrive, future; — s.m. One who, or that which, has yet to come ( = āne-hārā).

def: part. Enclosed, surrounded; covered (see next).
s.f. Order, method; returning, repetition; resolution.
adj. First, prior, foremost, chief, greatest, highest, best, excellent; — s.m. The first or earlier part, beginning; — adv. At first, in the beginning, in the first place or instance
subs: To remain or stand first in order; to maintain the first place
adv. From beginning to end, from first to last throughout
s.f. lit. 'First stage'; interment, obsequies; — awwal manzil pahuṅćānā, v.t. To perform (one's) obsequies, to bury.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: adv. At first, &c. = awwal, q.v. adj. First, prior, foremost, chief, greatest, highest, best, excellent; — s.m. The first or earlier part, beginning; — adv. At first, in the beginning, in the first place or instance
subs: To remain or stand first in order; to maintain the first place
adv. From beginning to end, from first to last throughout
s.f. lit. 'First stage'; interment, obsequies; — awwal manzil pahuṅćānā, v.t. To perform (one's) obsequies, to bury.

def: adj. First, former, ancient; — s.m. The ancients
subs: s.m. The ancients and the moderns.

def: s.f. Priority, preëminence, superiority, excellence.
adj. Unrepelled; unalarmed, not disconcerted.
part. adj. Unapparent, indistinct, invisible, unperceived, imperceptible, undetermined; — s.m. The Supreme Being or Universal Spirit; soul, nature, temperament.
adj. Not disturbed, composed, calm, firm.
adj. Not spread over or pervading the whole, not penetrating or permeating; special, peculiar.
s.f. Inadequate diffusion or extent (of a definition, &c.); — adj. Not diffusive, non-pervading.
adj. Undecided; without regulation, irregular.
part. adj. Not put in order, not regulated, irregular, undisciplined, unsteady.

adj. Not liable to change; unperishable, immutable, eternal; unwasted; economical, parsimonious; — s.m. An epithet of Vishṇu, or of Śiva; the Supreme Being or Universal Spirit; (in Gram.) an indeclinable word, particle.
adj. (in Gram.) Unchangeable, indeclinable; relating to an indeclinable word
part. (of ānā), Come
subs: adj. (f. āʼī-gaʼī), Come and gone, gone, past, done with, spent, got rid of, disposed of
s.m. pl. Casual visitors, unexpected guests, guests
v.n. To be come and gone, to be gone, done with, be a thing of the past; to be disposed of, be settled; to be got rid of, spent, lost, done away with.

def: s.f. Female attendant on children or on a lady, nurse, lady's maid, ayah.
s.f. Nurse, &c. = āyā, q.v. s.m. Income, revenue, receipts, gain, profit.
s.m. = āyat, q.v.
s.m. Income, revenue, receipts, gain, profit.
intj. Ha! O! ho! I say! hark you!
s.m. Cup, drinking-vessel.
s.f. Mane (of a horse); lock of hair (between a horse's ears).
s.m. Extension, expansion; length; breadth.
s.m. Grandees, nobles.
adj. & s.m. Unwise, ignorant, silly; — simpleton, dolt, fool (opp. of syānā).

ety: act. part. of āmadan; āy+anda; rt. Zend & S. i + ant
def: adj. & adv. Coming, future, subsequent, ensuing, next; — in future, for the future, hereafter
subs: adj. & s.m. Coming and going, passing to and fro
a traveller; — āʼinde-ko, adv. In future.

def: s.m. Effort, exertion, trouble, toil, labour; fatigue, exertion.
adj. Well-up, versed, skilled, proficient (in, -meṅ).
s.m. Command, order ( = ādeś).
s.m. = آيس āyasu, q.v. s.m. Command, order ( = ādeś).
s.f. Mark, sign; sentence or verse of the Qorʼān
subs: s.f. Mark, stop, pause (used in the Qorʼān only, and differing from a simple āyat in being a compulsory pause).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آیَه (āya), from Arabic آيَة (ʔāya).

def: adj. & s.m. Long, wide; oblong (field or figure); — an oblong figure, parallelogram.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آیَه (āya), from Arabic آيَة (ʔāya).

def: s.m. Sunshine ( = dhūp, q.v.). s.m. Incense, frankincense; any fragrant gum or resin (burnt before idols); vapour proceeding from fragrant gum or resin, aromatic vapour or smoke, perfume; — (also, in Hindī and Urdū) s.f. Sunlight, sunshine, heat of the sun, the sun; — prickly heat
subs: v.n. To be sunny, to be hot
dhūp ćaṛhānā, To burn incense (before an idol, &c.)
v.n. The sun to rise, or to be high
s.f. Sunshine and shade; — shot silk
s.m., or dhūp-dānī, s.f. Incensepot, censer; box for keeping incense
s.f. The burning of incense (before a Jain idol) on the tenth of the light fortnight of Bhādoṅ
v.t. To put in the sun (to dry, &c.), to sun
s.m. Offering of incense (to an idol)
s.m. Hot weather, the hot season
v.n. To bask in the sun
(or bāl) safaid karnā, 'To whiten one's hair in the sun'; to become gray-headed without gaining experience or wisdom; to pass life in ignorance
vulg. dhūp-br̤iksh, s.m. 'Incense-tree'; a species of pine, Pinus longifolia
s.f. A clouded sun, a very hot sun.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آیَه (āya), from Arabic آيَة (ʔāya).
